sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Girona FC comes into this match in strong form, having won their last three matches, including a 2-0 victory over Espanyol and a narrow 1-0 win against Osasuna. Getafe, on the other hand, is struggling, winless in their last seven games, with recent losses to Valencia (0-1) and Real Sociedad (1-2), highlighting defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Getafe has had the upper hand, winning both matches with scores of 2-1. Their most recent clash on October 31, 2025, saw Getafe triumph at home, suggesting they might have a tactical edge over Girona despite their current form slump.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Girona’s Vladyslav Vanat has been in fine form, scoring 4 goals in the last 5 matches, and could be a significant threat. Getafe misses key forward Borja Mayoral, which might impact their attacking output, though Luis Milla’s creativity (7 assists this season) remains a danger.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Girona FC struggles with goal-scoring (20 goals, 15th in La Liga) and has a leaky defense (34 conceded, 18th). Getafe is even worse offensively (15 goals, among the lowest), but their recent matches suggest they can capitalize on defensive errors, as seen in head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite Sofascore’s prediction favoring Getafe (44% away win chance), Girona’s recent form and home advantage at Estadi Montilivi could tilt the balance. However, Getafe’s historical edge in this fixture and Girona’s defensive frailties might result in a close, low-scoring contest.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline between Girona FC and Getafe remains level at 0-0. Neither side has managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Girona has dominated possession and shown more intent in the final third, but Getafe has looked dangerous on the counter and has had a higher expected goals figure, suggesting they’ve created better quality chances. Both teams have had their share of attacking opportunities, but a lack of clinical finishing has kept the game goalless so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Girona FC's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Girona FC has controlled much of the game with significant ball possession and a higher number of entries into the final third. Their approach has been methodical, focusing on building play through the middle and exploiting the wings. They’ve managed a shot on target and hit the woodwork once, indicating they’ve come close to scoring. However, their aerial duels and overall duels won percentages are concerning, as they’ve struggled to dominate physically. Defensive solidity has been decent, but they’ll need to convert their territorial advantage into goals in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Getafe's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Getafe has adopted a more pragmatic approach, ceding possession to Girona but looking sharp on the break. Their final third entries have been frequent despite lower possession, and they’ve shown a strong physical presence, winning a high percentage of duels and aerial challenges. While they haven’t registered a shot on target, their expected goals suggest they’ve had opportunities to test the Girona defense. Defensively, they’ve been organized, with several clearances and interceptions, but they’ll need to capitalize on counter-attacking chances to turn the game in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in attack during the first half started with Getafe showing early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes. However, Girona quickly took control, with sustained attacking spells dominating much of the half and showing consistent pressure in the opposition half. Their attacking momentum peaked significantly around the midway point, with relentless waves of pressure. Towards the end of the half, Getafe managed a brief resurgence with a dangerous attacking spell, but Girona regained control in the closing stages. The shifts in momentum suggest Girona has the upper hand in dictating play, but Getafe remains a threat on the counter.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Girona, the focus in the second half should be on improving their finishing and making better use of their dominance in possession. They need to be more clinical in front of goal and win more physical battles to assert greater control. Getafe, on the other hand, must maintain their defensive organization while being more decisive in their attacking transitions. Converting their counter-attacking opportunities into shots on target will be crucial if they are to steal a result. Both teams will likely make tactical adjustments to address these areas after the break.
